category_title: Non-verbal students with moderate to severe cognitive impairments who use modified instrumentation
scenario_title: Social boundaries and peer interactions during transitions
minimal_context: Zack is transitioning to the circle to sit with his classmates during an inclusive fourth-grade music class.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Zack, let's transition to the circle and sit with your classmates.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Walks to the circle with a classmate and reaches out his arms to try and hug her.)

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (When the classmate firmly says 'Zack, no hug' and touches his outstretched arms to hold him back, he ducks under her arms and hugs her anyway.)

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Please sit down and get ready.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Swipes the classmate's recorder cleaning stick and starts to play 'keep away' with it, ignoring her verbal cues to give it back while looking for a reaction from the class.)

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: Zack, let's transition to the circle and sit with your classmates.

STUDENT: (Walks to the circle with a classmate and reaches out his arms to try and hug her.)

TUTOR: Listen to your classmate.

STUDENT: (When the classmate firmly says 'Zack, no hug' and touches his outstretched arms to hold him back, he ducks under her arms and hugs her anyway.)

TUTOR: Please sit down and get ready.

STUDENT: (Swipes the classmate's recorder cleaning stick and starts to play 'keep away' with it, ignoring her verbal cues to give it back while looking for a reaction from the class.)
